reviews.review_msg: The professor was nice, she tried her best to engage the class & always stopped to ask for questions. Sometimes, lectures were confusing since she skipped steps. Quizzes were helpful because they forced me to study and review lol. Midterms were fair, but the final exam was way harder. Luckily, she adjusted the grades :) +crib sheets were useful!

course wasn't too difficult, practice w/ IQ assignments as they are identical to exam questions. I liked this class a lot more than calc1 (grade:C) and found it a lot simpler. she does skip over important slides in class, but she posts completed slides after. not the worst, not the best. PREPARE FOR FINAL!!!

The course isn't too bad however it's very fast. There's a lot of work including weekly in class quizzes and it's hard to stay on top of it. Lectures were pointless you're gonna have to teach yourself everything. The midterms were easy but the final was hard and it's worth 50% of your grade.

She was nice, but she would sometimes skip over important steps when explaining a problem, which was confusing. She has summaries for each topic in the solutions to her problem sets which were helpful. The weekly online assignmemts arent too bad as long as you start working on them early. The weekly quizzes arent given enough time to complete.

Dr. Macpherson is a lovely person, but the lectures were horrible. The lecture videos were confusing and difficult to follow. Midterms and finals did not reflect what was taught at all. I'd recommend using YouTube to learn everything. I only passed bc the class was curved.

Math 154 and math 155 are two of the worst classes I've ever taken it's like they want you to fail. Prof.Macpherson tried her best and she was nice and all but still doesnt rlly know how to teach. you have to teach yourself EVERYTHING. first midterm is as easy as it gets. Lots of hw. Pre lecture videos were useless. COME TO LECTURE dont fall behind
Lectures not helpful. Fast paced. Reached out for help, was not helpful. Get ready to teach yourself via YouTube

It's always a race, impossible to keep up with the notes, much less learn in lectures. Quizzes and exams are ridiculously packed for the time aloted. The first midterms when so bad she didn't release the average.

Ailene was one of the best professors I had at SFU. Her passion for math shines through in her clear lecture notes and dedication to student success. She ensures we understand concepts and encourages participation. The hate comes from students who don't know how to study.

she is a really good prof and she is super sweet. most of these negative reviews are coming from whiny first year students that are still getting the hang of university. she was caring and helped if you needed some help. she does make some mistakes but it happens if you are a new prof and if you put in the effort you will do well, so stop slacking.

AVOID AT ALL COSTS! She threatened to fail me at the beginning of the semester and did so despite my quizzes coming back with passing grades. Had to change my major halfway through my degree because of the grade given.

I think Dr. Macpherson got so much hate for no reason. She was a caring professor and just wanted her students to succeed! The lectures may have been confusing at times but everyone makes mistakes. I highly would recommend taking math 154 with Dr.macpherson!

I think everyone was too harsh on MacPherson. While her lectures are confusing, her mini-lectures were actually very helpful. I would go to lectures just for the iclicker and do other work during that time. WATCH THE MINI LECTURES. I also found the exams to be very simple and straightforward with no trick questions. Plus she allows cheat sheets

I got an A in that class but no thanks to the prof. she explained simple topics in a confusing manner and I was only able to get a good grade due to taking AP Calc in high school and teaching myself outside of class.
